sources.name: MOERI KVLCC2 reference hull (SIMMAN / simman2014.dk / simman2008.dk / SIMMAN2020)
sources.format: Web tables + downloadable IGES hull/rudder/propeller geometry, offset files, condition sheets (PDF/zip)
sources.freshness: Geometry stable since 2008; refreshed each workshop cycle (2008/2014/2019/2020). Reference hull, not a live-fleet feed.
sources.notes: Best single fully-defined public VLCC: gives L/B/T, block coeff, displacement, KG, and roll radius of gyration (~0.4B in air), bilge keels, rudder/propeller geometry. Motion/seakeeping + maneuvering validation data from MARIN/MOERI. Ideal seed record for a class-particulars + mass-properties + RAO schema. KCS (container) and KVLCC2 are the canonical open benchmark hulls.

sources.name: Wikipedia ship-class articles (Oil tanker, Aframax, Suezmax, VLCC/ULCC, LNG carrier, Q-Max/Q-Flex, LPG carrier)
sources.freshness: Continuously edited; class DWT/dimension bands current
sources.notes: Good for class-band definitions (DWT ranges, typical LOA/beam/draft, Q-Max 266,000 m3 etc.). Use as orientation, not authoritative per-ship values; chase the cited primary sources for a database.

sources.name: Clarksons — 'A guide to bulk/tanker vessel sizes' (free glossary)
sources.notes: Authoritative size-band definitions from the leading broker. Note: this free glossary is the only open part; Clarksons SIN/Registers fleet data with per-ship particulars is paywalled (see paywalled_gaps).

sources.name: MarineInsight — Guide to Ship Sizes / Gas Carrier design articles
sources.notes: Useful narrative + typical-dimension tables across MR/LR1/Aframax/Suezmax/VLCC/ULCC and LNG/LPG segments. Secondary source; cross-check numbers.

sources.name: Shipbuilder product pages — Japan Marine United, Hyundai/HD KSOE, Samsung Heavy, Hanwha Ocean (ex-DSME)
sources.freshness: Reflects current standard designs (e.g., 174,000 m3 membrane LNGC; 82k/70k/39k/18k m3 LPGC)
sources.notes: Builder-stated standard-class particulars (LOA, beam, draft, cargo capacity, containment type, BOR). Authoritative for as-designed standard newbuilds but coverage is per-builder and marketing-oriented; mass properties and RAOs not published.

sources.name: OCIMF publications — Recommendations for Oil Tanker Manifolds & Associated Equipment; Mooring Equipment Guidelines (MEG4)
sources.format: Purchased PDF/print standards; catalogue is public
sources.notes: Closest 'deck data' analogue for tankers: standardized manifold spacing/height/strength and deck-arrangement geometry. The standards themselves are paid; the catalogue and many summaries are public. Trading tankers have no significant deck cranes (only hose-handling/provision cranes), so true crane capacity curves are largely N/A for this scope.

sources.name: SIGTTO / ICS-OCIMF-SIGTTO-CDI Ship-to-Ship Transfer Guide (2nd ed. 2025) and gas-carrier manifold guidance
sources.format: Publication (paid) + freely circulated IMO INF papers/summaries (PDF)
sources.notes: Gas-carrier manifold layout/strength/fittings and hose-handling crane practice for LNG/LPG. An IMO PPR INF paper summarizing the 2025 edition is publicly downloadable via ICS; the full guide is paid.

sources.name: DNV-RP-C205 'Environmental conditions and environmental loads'
sources.format: PDF standard (free download after DNV account registration)
sources.notes: The authoritative reference for wave/wind/current/tide modelling and environmental loads used to set metocean design criteria. DNV RPs are downloadable at no charge after registering a free DNV account; copies also circulate on third-party sites (unofficial).

sources.name: BOEM Metocean Characterization Recommended Practices (DNV GL doc 10039663-HOU-01)
sources.freshness: Published report (US offshore wind focus, methods transferable)
sources.notes: Fully open government PDF on metocean characterization methodology (extremes, hindcast, contours). Useful free companion to DNV-RP-C205 for criteria methodology, though oriented to US offshore wind sites.

sources.name: Equasis (Electronic Quality Shipping Information System)
sources.notes: Free-after-registration per-ship lookup: IMO no., type, GT, year, flag, MMSI/callsign. Carries identity/class/safety particulars, NOT full naval-arch dimensions (limited LOA/beam) and no mass properties or RAOs. Good identity backbone to key a vessel database.

sources.name: EMSA THETIS (Paris MoU PSC public search)
sources.notes: Public lookup of ship name/IMO/type/GT/year/flag/company. Same caveat as Equasis: identity + tonnage, not detailed hull dimensions, mass properties, or motions.

sources.name: Maritime academic / open hydrodynamics literature (ResearchGate, ScienceDirect topic pages, arXiv NEMOH/Capytaine studies, J. Marine Sci. Eng. open-access papers on S-VLCC seakeeping)
sources.notes: Many open-access papers give VLCC/tanker heave/roll/pitch RAOs and the input mass properties (radii of gyration, KG) for KVLCC2-class hulls; figures often need digitizing. Good for filling the RAO layer where commercial seakeeping reports are unavailable. Open BEM tools (NEMOH/Capytaine) let you regenerate RAOs from KVLCC2 geometry.

paywalled_gaps: Clarksons Shipping Intelligence Network (SIN) / Clarksons Research World Fleet Register — detailed per-ship and per-class particulars (LOA, beam, depth, draft, DWT, cargo capacity, builder, engine). Paid subscription; only the free size-band glossary is public.
IHS Markit / S&P Global Maritime (Sea-web, World Register of Ships, ex-Lloyd's Register Fairplay) — the most complete authoritative per-ship dimensions and characteristics database. Paywalled.
S&P Global Commodity Insights / Petrodata (offshore & tanker fleet/positions) — paywalled.

Classification society ship registers with full naval-arch detail (e.g., ABS Record, DNV/LR vessel registers) — basic identity is free, but full hydrostatic/structural particulars are restricted to owners/class.
OCIMF standards (Manifold Recommendations, MEG4) and SIGTTO full publications — the standards documents themselves must be purchased (catalogue + IMO summaries are public).

Mass properties (lightship weight, vertical/longitudinal CoG, full radii of gyration in all axes) and as-built motion RAOs for specific commercial hulls — these are owner/yard-proprietary and effectively never public except for reference hulls like KVLCC2 or in academic studies; this is the largest genuine data gap for a real-fleet database.
recommended_priority: MOERI KVLCC2 reference hull (SIMMAN / simman2014.dk / simman2008.dk / SIMMAN2020)
Clarksons — 'A guide to bulk/tanker vessel sizes' (free glossary)
Shipbuilder product pages — Japan Marine United, Hyundai/HD KSOE, Samsung Heavy, Hanwha Ocean (ex-DSME)
EMSA THETIS (Paris MoU PSC public search)
BOEM Metocean Characterization Recommended Practices (DNV GL doc 10039663-HOU-01)
Maritime academic / open hydrodynamics literature (KVLCC2 seakeeping RAOs, NEMOH/Capytaine)
MarineInsight — Guide to Ship Sizes / Gas Carrier design articles
